title: Gold ETFs vs gold mutual funds: Key differences in returns, costs, taxation and SIPs
summary: Gold ETFs invest directly in physical gold and related instruments, while gold mutual funds buy units of gold ETFs. While both fall under the mutual fund category regulated by AMFI, they differ significantly in terms of investment structure, taxation, costs, liquidity, and investment approach.

title: ITR-2 filing begins: Who should file, who cannot – Check eligibility, exclusions, deadlines

Eligibility criteria are specified, while ITR-1 and ITR-4 have also been made available for small and medium taxpayers.

title: EPFO 3.0: Want to withdraw 100% of your PF money? Check the latest withdrawal rules
summary: EPFO subscribers will soon be able to access their provident fund savings directly through UPI, reducing paperwork and waiting times. Members can withdraw 50% to 75% of their EPF balance. The new system aims for a paperless experience, with testing completed and rollout expected soon.

title: US degree now costs more than a 2BHK in Bengaluru and Gurgaon – How parents are prioritising
summary: Indian parents face emotional and financial challenges sending children abroad for education, with costs rising to ₹5 crore by 2036 due to rupee depreciation and tuition hikes. Despite high expenses, 90% still prefer overseas studies, relying on long-term investments to ease the financial burden.

title: 8th Pay Commission latest update: Big salary hike unlikely, why Centre may settle for a moderate pay revision
summary: Speculation about the 8th Pay Commission grows as central government employees await long-promised salary revisions. Reports indicate the Centre may not meet all union demands, including a higher fitment factor, raising doubts about substantial pay hikes amid rising inflation pressures.

title: Bank holiday today, May 28: Are SBI, HDFC Bank and other banks open or closed for Bakrid/Eid al-Adha?
summary: Most banks will be closed on May 28, 2026, for Bakrid/Eid al-Adha, a gazetted public holiday. Central government offices will also observe this day off, replacing the previously scheduled holiday on May 27.
